text.title: Agency Niche Positioning Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Agency Niche Positioning` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The chosen niche MUST be one rung narrower than the founder's instinct (e.g., not 'SaaS' but 'B2B SaaS in legaltech with seed-to-Series-A'). Broader picks are rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Founders default to too-broad picks to hedge revenue; one-rung-narrower forces a real positioning bet that the market can perceive.

rule.statement: Before the ADR is signed, founder MUST write a 'say-no list' of project types they will refuse for the next 90 days. List has ≥ 5 concrete project types.
rule.rationale: Without a pre-committed say-no list, the agency drifts back to opportunism within 30 days; explicit refusal types create accountability.

rule.statement: All public portfolio items MUST be re-titled / re-described to centre the chosen niche before the new positioning launches publicly.
rule.rationale: Mixed-vertical portfolios actively contradict the niche claim; repackaging is non-negotiable pre-launch work.

rule.statement: The ADR MUST name a single human owner accountable for the positioning over the next quarter. Group handles forbidden.
rule.rationale: Group-owned positioning never gets enforced; named owner has lever to refuse out-of-niche deals at sales review.

rule.statement: The ADR MUST set a 90-day review trigger with concrete metrics (inbound % from niche, average deal size in niche, churn from niche) and a stop-loss condition that reverts the positioning if breached.
rule.rationale: Positioning experiments without stop-loss decay; quarterly review with named metrics surfaces failures before runway burns.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
